slides.lead: Demo presentation of this template. All text is sample (dummy) copy and exists only to showcase the styles.
slides.notes: Opening slide. This is where the event name, a one-line subtitle and the bottom meta row go. Highlight one word with em for the accent color.
Team slide. Uses initials in a colored circle instead of photos, so it needs no images.

slides.lead: Three parts, with room for questions between them.
slides.notes: Agenda as three large tiles. Good at the start of a block.

slides.lead: The market, our orders and the biggest bottlenecks.
slides.notes: Section divider between blocks. Large heading on a colored gradient.

slides.lead: Cravings do not wait. The winner is whoever connects hungry people, local kitchens and couriers in the fewest minutes.
slides.footnote: // illustrative slide with an enlarged image
slides.notes: Hero slide: large heading, bold illustration, short caption. Good for a key point or a chapter.

items: average delivery time across the city this quarter
repeat orders within thirty days of a first order
lower fees for restaurants versus the big platforms
bullets: Measure impact, not activity: speed and repeat rate decide the business.
Start where demand is dense and couriers are already nearby.
A small zone with a clear metric beats a city-wide plan without one.
slides.notes: KPI dashboard: three cards with an icon, a large number and a label, supporting bullets below.

slides.lead: From a tap to a doorstep in four moves.
bullets: A customer taps once and pays in seconds.
We match the closest free courier instantly.
The kitchen sees the ticket the moment it lands.
Live tracking until the bag reaches the door.
slides.notes: Two-column slide: bullets with a strong lead word on the left, illustration on the right. Text scales itself.

slides.lead: Same delivery, two very different cost curves.
cards: Huge reach, but 30 percent fees gut restaurant margins.
Full control, yet hard to staff and scale on demand.
Shared courier pool with fees kept low and transparent.
Own riders at peak, partner couriers for the long tail.
slides.notes: A 2x2 grid of cards. Good for comparisons or listing variants.

slides.lead: Six building blocks behind a fast delivery.
cards: Couriers assigned in under a second near the kitchen.
Customers watch the order move in real time.
Demand-shaped delivery areas keep times tight.
Transparent fees that restaurants can actually plan around.
Repeat orders rewarded without spammy discounts.
slides.notes: A 3x2 grid with an icon in each card header. Great for a tools or capabilities overview.

items: One new neighborhood a month with proven unit economics.
A viral dish and dense zones compound orders fast.
Demand levels off, so we double down on retention and margin.
slides.notes: Numbered cards with small trend charts and an illustration on the right. Ideal for three possible futures.

slides.lead: Five steps, each with a clear output.
slides.notes: A horizontal flow of steps with arrows. Each box has a heading and a small caption.

slides.quote: Nobody remembers the app. They remember whether the food arrived hot and on time.
slides.by: Sample quote to showcase the style
slides.notes: Quote slide with a bold left rule. Keep the attribution small.

slides.lead: Simple, but easy to forget under pressure.
bullets: Every zone has a named person accountable for it.
You know up front how success is measured.
Happy couriers deliver faster and stay longer.
slides.notes: A plain bullet slide with a strong lead word. Text scales up automatically to fill the space.

slides.lead: A fast first delivery earns the second order.
slides.notes: Statement slide: one big sentence centered on the page. Use it for a key message.

slides.lead: What to do this week, this month and this quarter.
slides.notes: Second divider, opening the practical part.

slides.h1: Order to door in under a minute.
slides.lead: We switch to the app and place a real order live.
slides.footnote: // placeholder slide for a demo or a break
slides.notes: Placeholder slide. Centered heading, ideal for a live demo, a break or questions.
Closing slide in the style of the title. Contact and call to action.
